data.title: Comparison Between Topical Ketoconazole 2% Cream and Topical Adapalene 2% Cream in the Treatment of Mild Comedonal and Papulopustular Acne, a Randomized Controlled Trial
data.brief_summary:
objective: is to assess the efficacy of topical ketoconazole 2% cream in comparison with topical retinoid in the treatment of mild comedonal and papulopustular acne to determine if ketoconazole can be an alternative to topical retinoid with less side effects and more compliance.

Patients and methods: 52 patients with mild comedonal and papulopustular acne will be randomized into 2 groups and instructed to apply randomly either topical KTZ 2% cream or topical retinoid cream for a period of 12 we
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Patients with mild comedonal or papulopustular acne with a GEA score from 1-2 on the face.
* Both sexes.
* Age: adolescents and adults.
* Skin type 3 and 4.

Exclusion Criteria:

* • Patients using, will use during the study, or discontinued less than 4 weeks before study base
endpoints: To study the efficacy of topical ketoconazole 2% cream in treatment of mild to moderate acne
